Role Scope and Responsibilities

Customer, Contract & Deal Financial Analysis
• Perform and document customer‑ and contract‑level financial analyses, including margin analysis, deal profitability, price/mix impact, and variance analysis.
• Support deal reviews, pricing decisions, and contract evaluations, ensuring financial soundness and compliance with internal policies.
• Translate financial data into clear, actionable insights to support commercial decision‑making and negotiations.

Business Partnering
• Act as a finance partner to MEA commercial, sales, and marketing teams, supporting customer strategies and commercial initiatives.
• Provide financial support for new business, renewals, and pricing actions, including scenario analysis and risk assessment.
• Influence outcomes through strong financial acumen and data‑driven storytelling, without formal authority.

Performance Management & Reporting
• Support monitoring of customer profitability, pricing realization, and commercial KPIs.
• Prepare and review performance dashboards, reports, and analyses for CAF leadership and commercial stakeholders.
• Identify performance gaps, risks, and opportunities, recommending corrective actions and improvement initiatives.

Governance & Control
• Ensure financial governance and compliance in deal and customer financial processes, in line with internal policies and control frameworks.
• Support internal and external audit requests related to customer finance and commercial activities, as required.
• Maintain accurate documentation of assumptions, analyses, and decision rationales.
• Support internal and external audit requirements as needed.
• Maintain and enhance documentation for financial processes, analyses, and methodologies.

Process Optimization & Capability Building
• Identify opportunities for process improvements, standardization, and automation within CAF activities.
• Collaborate closely with FP&A, Accounting, and P&A teams to ensure data consistency and end‑to‑end financial visibility.
• Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives aligned with MEA Finance Transformation objectives.

Independence & Decision‑Making
• Operates with moderate autonomy and clear guidance from senior CAF leadership.
• Manages assigned analyses and workstreams independently while escalating issues appropriately.
• Proactively suggests improvements and solutions to enhance commercial decision‑making.

Qualifications & Experience

Education
• University degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field.
• Professional certification (CMA, ACCA, CPA, or equivalent) is an advantage.

Experience
• 3–5 years of relevant experience in financial analysis, commercial finance, FP&A, or controlling.
• Experience working in a matrix, multinational environment; MEA exposure is preferred.

Technical & Professional Skills
• Strong analytical, critical‑thinking, and problem‑solving skills.
• Advanced proficiency in Excel; experience with Power BI or similar analytics tools is an advantage.
• Solid understanding of profitability analysis, pricing, and financial fundamentals.
• Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver accurate outputs under tight timelines.

Behavioral Competencies
• Commercially minded with a proactive and solution‑oriented approach.
• Clear communicator able to work effectively with sales and cross‑functional stakeholders.
• Detail‑oriented, resilient, and comfortable operating in a fast‑changing environment.

Our Commitment to a Culture of Inclusion & Belonging
Ecolab is committed to fair and equal treatment of associates and applicants and furthering the principles of Equal Opportunity to Employment. We will recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement based on individual qualifications and job performance in all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement. Ecolab will not discriminate against any associate or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin,citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, or disability.

Located in Chinchwad, the firm focuses on client-centric solutions while upholding integrity and accuracy in financial practices. The team is committed to fostering talent and providing valuable learning opportunities for aspiring professionals.

This is a full-time, on-site role for an Article Trainee located in Chinchwad. The role involves assisting in audit and assurance assignments, preparing financial statements, filing tax returns, and supporting compliance-related activities. The Article Trainee will also engage in research, data analysis, and client communication while gaining practical exposure to various facets of accounting, taxation, and business advisory services. Financial Analysis & Insights

• Act as a lead finance partner to MEA stakeholders across commercial, supply chain, and functional teams.

• Identify and lead opportunities for process improvements, standardization, and automation across finance activities.
• Actively contribute to and shape MEA Finance Transformation priorities, bringing best practices and forward‑looking solutions.

Independence & Decision‑Making
• Operates with very high autonomy, professional judgment, and accountability.
• Leads complex analytical workstreams and cross‑functional initiatives independently.

Qualifications & Experience

Education
• University degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field.
• Professional certification (CMA, ACCA, CPA, or equivalent) is an advantage.

Experience
• 7–10 years of relevant experience in financial analysis, FP&A, commercial finance, or controlling.
• Experience working in a matrix, multinational environment; MEA exposure is preferred.

Technical & Professional Skills
• Advanced analytical, critical‑thinking, and problem‑solving capabilities.
• Advanced proficiency in Excel; experience with Power BI or similar tools is an advantage.
• Deep understanding of performance management, financial controls, and business drivers.
• Strong ability to manage complexity, ambiguity, and tight timelines in a fast‑paced environment.

Behavioral Competencies
• Strong strategic and commercial acumen with a challenging and forward‑thinking mindset.

Position Summary:

The Finance Planning and Analyst (FP&A) at NielsenIQ is a key member of the FP&A Hub team, responsible for supporting the local team in managing financial planning processes. This role involves integrating and developing financial processes that align with both revenue and expense goals, participating in strategic planning, and contributing to global and local strategies. The successful candidate will demonstrate proactive collaboration with peers and leaders and contribute their analytical expertise to drive financial success.

• Variance Analysis: Contribute to monthly variance analysis to identify and explain financial performance deviations.
• SIP Payment Review: Ensure SIP payment information aligns with agreed-upon data.
• Expense Budget Management: Manage expense budgets and ensure leaders remain on plan.
• Headcount Forecasting: Prepare and track headcount forecast dashboards.

• Collaboration: Work closely with peers, leaders, and other functional areas to gather necessary data and provide financial insights.
• Continuous Improvement: Identify opportunities for process improvements and efficiencies within financial planning and analysis activities.
• Compliance: Ensure all financial activities comply with internal policies and external regulations.

data.job_title: Chartered Accountant - Finance & Global Accounting Analyst (Pune)
data.job_description:
PayGlocal is a global payments company that fosters global commerce. Our suite of offerings facilitate cross-border payments and solve the challenges surrounding it through three main pillars - the business, the experience and the associated risk.

Founded by finance veterans and experts, our understanding of merchant business, international business, security and regulatory standards help build impactful payment solutions. We partner with large enterprises and export merchants for seamless customer payment journeys.

Be a part of lasting innovation - create new products, new processes and new standards. Join us to help us shape the future of payments.

Position Summary

As a Chartered Accountant – Finance & Global Accounting , you will play a critical role in strengthening the finance backbone of a fast-growing cross-border payments business. You will be responsible for managing multi-entity accounting, supporting cross-border transaction flows, ensuring regulatory compliance across jurisdictions (India, US, UAE), and enabling accurate financial reporting.

This role is ideal for a qualified CA (0–2 years’ experience) who is detail-oriented, analytically strong, and eager to gain exposure to global accounting practices, fintech operations, and scalable financial processes in a high-growth environment.

What will you work on?

- Multi-Entity Accounting: Assist in preparation and finalization of books of accounts across multiple entities and jurisdictions (India, US, UAE, etc.).
- Financial Closures: Support month-end and year-end closing activities, including reconciliations, journal entries, and financial statement preparation.
- Inter-Company Transactions: Manage inter-company accounting, reconciliations, and cross-border charge validations.
- Transfer Pricing Support: Assist in transfer pricing documentation and ensure compliance with inter-company pricing regulations.
- Regulatory & Audit Compliance: Support statutory audits, tax audits, RBI/FEMA/GST compliance, and financial regulatory requirements.
- Cross-Border & FX Accounting: Work on cross-border payment flows, foreign exchange accounting, and revenue reconciliations.
- MIS & Reporting: Prepare MIS reports, variance analysis, financial dashboards, and performance tracking reports for leadership.
- Treasury Coordination: Assist in fund flow tracking, banking coordination, and liquidity monitoring.
- Process Improvement: Contribute to finance automation initiatives, ERP implementation support, and SOP documentation.
- Stakeholder Coordination: Work closely with auditors, consultants, tax advisors, and internal business teams.

Qualifications & Experience

- Qualification: Chartered Accountant (CA) – Qualified.
- Experience: 0–2 years of relevant experience (Articleship experience preferred).
- Accounting Knowledge: Strong understanding of accounting standards, financial statements, and reconciliation processes.
- Analytical Skills: Solid analytical and problem-solving capabilities with attention to detail.
- Technical Skills: Advanced proficiency in MS Excel; familiarity with ERP/accounting tools such as Zoho, Tally, NetSuite, QuickBooks, etc.
- Communication: Strong written and verbal communication skills with ability to coordinate across teams and external stakeholders.
- Work Environment Fit: Ability to thrive in a fast-paced fintech/startup environment with high ownership and accountability.

Additional Information:

• Exposure : Opportunity to work on global accounting practices across India, US, and UAE entities.

• Career Growth : High-ownership role with leadership exposure in a rapidly scaling fintech company.

• Learning & Development : Direct exposure to cross-border payments, regulatory frameworks, and scalable finance processes.

• Benefits : Health insurance and standard company benefits.

Target Profile:

• A recently qualified CA looking to build expertise in global and cross-border accounting.

• Strong interest in fintech, payments, and international finance.

• Detail-oriented professional with strong reconciliation and compliance mindset.

• Comfortable handling multi-entity accounting structures and regulatory complexity.

Position Description
The CITICS CLSA Equity Derivatives team (EQD) offers its clients a wide range of products and services meeting different needs like risk management, investment return enhancement, financing, and cross-border market access. EQD’s products and services include Prime Services, Derivative Products, Quantitative Investment Strategies (QIS), and Market Making.
EQD Quants is a team of quantitative analysts who specialize in quantitative finance and technology and work closely with EQD traders, sales and structuring teams. EQD quants design and implement EQDs e-trading infrastructure, market making systems, and trading systems for OTC non-linear, QIS, OTC prime and delta one.
This role will be primarily focused on researching and designing market making and trading strategies for EQD in India and other Asia-Pacific markets. The hire will report locally to the head of EQD Quant India and work closely with (a) EQD quants in Hong Kong and London, (b) EQD traders and structurers in Hong Kong, Singapore, Tokyo, London and New York and (c) EQD developers in India and other markets. The position will report to the EQD Quant India head in Mumbai.
Key Areas of Responsibilities
The day-to-day responsibilities of the EQD Quant will include—
• Analysis of market microstructure data to identify actionable trading signals for equities and derivatives in India and other Asia-Pac markets

• Analysis of order book data to identify actionable trading signals for equities and derivatives in India and other Asia-Pac markets

• Research and development of trading models using real-time and historical trade and quote data for equities and derivatives in India and other Asia-Pac markets

• Back-testing of the above strategies

• Understanding and enhancing EQD’s trading strategies for India and other Asia-Pac markets

• Working with EQD developers and the firm’s technology teams to implement trading strategies and optimizing code

• Understanding and contributing to EQD’s trading infrastructure

• Monitoring live performance of EQD’s trading strategies for continuous enhancement of signal detection and performance robustness

Requirements
• A bachelor’s degree in engineering from a top-tier Indian institute (e.g., the top IITs) and/or a master’s or PhD degree in a quantitative field (Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Quantitative Finance, etc.) from a top-tier Indian or foreign university

• 6+ years of professional industry experience, ideally in research or development of trading strategies

• Deep knowledge of options, futures and other equity derivatives, especially in India

• Deep knowledge of market microstructure and exchange protocols in India/Asia-Pac markets

• Advanced knowledge of low-latency systems and real-time data processing
